title: Sample and apply input per simulation tick
description: Capture input and apply it within the simulation step it belongs to, buffering events so none are lost between frames.
body: Use when player actions drive simulation. Do not read input only at render time, and do not drop events that arrive between frames. Actions: buffer raw input events as they arrive, then consume them inside the fixed-timestep update so each action maps to a definite tick; account for the input-to-photon latency budget. Validation signals include every quick tap registering (no missed inputs) and consistent action timing regardless of frame rate. Risk boundary: reading input at render time couples actions to frame rate and can miss fast inputs or add a frame of lag — in competitive or deterministic games this corrupts both feel and replay correctness.
